name: Hotel Romazzino, a Luxury Collection Hotel

content.verdict: Romazzino is the beach-first sibling of Costa Smeralda's three Luxury Collection grande dames: 93 whitewashed rooms stepped down a hillside directly onto a crescent of the softest white sand on the Italian coast. The vibe is looser and sunnier than Cala di Volpe or Pitrizza — families use it in August, but honeymooners who time it for late May, June, or September get one of the most romantic beaches in the Mediterranean largely to themselves. For couples who prioritize feet-in-the-water mornings and aperitivo on the sand over the Porto Cervo scene, this is the Costa Smeralda pick.
content.best_room: A Premium Sea View Room on the second or third floor puts you eye-level with the beach and the pink granite of Punta Capriccioli. The one-bedroom Junior Suite with sea-view terrace is the classic honeymoon room; the Romazzino Suite with private pool is the blow-the-budget option.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and toes-in-sand welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from Olbia Costa Smeralda in about 35 minutes. Check in, unpack, and walk straight to the beach for a welcome aperitivo at the Barracuda beach bar. Dinner at the main restaurant terrace with sunset views.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Full beach day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Sunbed service with umbrella on the hotel's private stretch of Romazzino beach. Long Vermentino lunch at the Beach Club, afternoon paddleboard tour of the small islands offshore, spa couple's treatment before dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Boat to Capriccioli and Mortorio
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private half-day charter from the beach. Anchor between the two Capriccioli beaches for a swim, continue to Isola di Mortorio for snorkeling, return for a late lunch at the hotel.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Porto Cervo and Spiaggia del Principe
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning drive (15 min) to Spiaggia del Principe, arguably the most beautiful beach on the coast. Back to Porto Cervo for lunch at Il Pescatore, afternoon shopping, and dinner in-hotel at the Romazzino Grill.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Phi Beach sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Beach morning, long lunch at the hotel, mid-afternoon drive to Baja Sardinia for sunset cocktails at Phi Beach (reservation essential) — the rock-amphitheater beach club is a Costa Smeralda rite of passage.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Inland Gallura wine day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Escape the coast for a day: drive 45 minutes inland to Surrau or Siddùra winery for a Vermentino tasting and lunch among cork oaks. Back to Romazzino for a quiet beach sunset.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Last breakfast on the terrace
itinerary_7_nights.description: Long breakfast, final swim, and the hotel car to Olbia. Many couples add two nights at a smaller Gallura property or in Alghero to round out the trip.

caveats: Much more family-oriented than Pitrizza in August — the kids' club and beach can feel busy in high summer. Shoulder season is markedly different.
Open seasonally (roughly mid-May to early October). The hotel closes completely in winter.
The hillside layout means stairs between room, pool, and beach. Request a lower-floor room if mobility matters.

faqs.question: Which of the three Luxury Collection Sardinia hotels is best for beach lovers?
faqs.answer: Romazzino, clearly — it is the only one directly on a long white-sand beach. Pitrizza has a tiny cove, Cala di Volpe sits on a bay without soft sand.

faqs.question: Is it suitable for a honeymoon if it has a kids' club?
faqs.answer: Yes, especially in June or September. The beach is large enough that you rarely feel surrounded, and the adult areas (spa, grill, beach bar) have their own atmosphere.

faqs.question: Can we use Pitrizza and Cala di Volpe facilities?
faqs.answer: Yes — cross-use of restaurants and beaches at all three sister hotels is included, and a free shuttle runs between them most of the day.

faqs.question: What is the sea like?
faqs.answer: Shallow, turquoise, with powder-soft white sand. One of the calmest and most child- and swim-friendly beaches on the coast, which makes it equally wonderful for couples who want to float for hours.

faqs.question: Do we need a rental car?
faqs.answer: Helpful but not essential. The hotel shuttle covers Porto Cervo and sister hotels. A car lets you explore Spiaggia del Principe, Liscia Ruja, and inland Gallura.

faqs.question: Best month for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: Early-to-mid June or the first two weeks of September — warm sea, long days, moderate crowds, and prices noticeably below peak August.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — Hotel Romazzino

Buongiorno,

We are planning our honeymoon for [DATES] and would love to stay 6 to 7 nights at Romazzino. Could you share best-available rates for a Premium Sea View Room or Junior Suite with sea-view terrace?

Also:
1. Are any honeymoon packages available (champagne, couple's spa treatment, celebratory dinner)?
2. Can we reserve sunbeds on the beach for the length of our stay?
3. Can you arrange a private boat charter directly from the beach?
4. What are the earliest check-in and latest checkout times possible for our arrival and departure days?

Many thanks,
[Your names]
